subject: Indiana's Party of Purpose
body:
In a busy election year like 2018, it’s easy to get distracted with the latest crazy thing Democrats are saying. But, even in election years like... | | |
---
| |
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=48125b4283&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| **_Indiana's Party of Purpose_** In a busy election year like 2018, it’s easy to get distracted with the latest crazy thing Democrats are saying. But, even in election years like this, our Republican elected officials are still hard at work from the Courthouse to the Statehouse serving our fellow Hoosiers and tackling the biggest issues we face as a state -- and it's important that we continue to shine a spotlight on their great work!

One great example of this work is the people-focused leadership that **Governor Eric Holcomb** is delivering for Hoosiers. Just yesterday, Governor Holcomb was at Stark Industries, a high-tech manufacturing facility in Terre Haute, to [unveil a new state office](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=f217f7d949&e=1acd9bb3d6) – the Office of Apprenticeship & Work-Based Learning – which will be laser-focused on doubling the number of Hoosiers who participate in apprenticeships and other work-based learning programs by 2020.

With upwards of 80,000 great job openings available across the state, Governor Holcomb understands that we need to make sure that every Hoosier has the opportunity to skill-up to fill high-demand, high-wage jobs, and we do that by maximizing apprenticeships and work-based learning programs for Hoosiers at all stages of their career.

That’s while **Senator Todd Young** is focusing on tackling another major issue that is facing Hoosiers – [lowering the rising cost of health care](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=74319469a3&e=1acd9bb3d6). He’s doing that by asking specific questions of all stakeholders involved – like hospitals, health care providers, and major insurance providers – about price-driving factors such as regulatory barriers, big data, and pricing transparency. Once we have these clear answers, Senator Young will work to promote legislation that will address changes that make our health care more affordable.

And it doesn’t stop there. Throughout our state, our elected Republican officials are making a real difference in Hoosiers’ lives. As we continue through this election season, I’d encourage you to pick up the torch for local Republicans in your community. Tell the story about how Republicans are working to build a better life for all Hoosiers and have made our party Indiana's true Party of Purpose.

-Kyle Hupfer

_Photo: This week, Chairman Kyle Hupfer joined Senator Jim Merritt to speak at the Marion County Republican Club about the work** _your_** Indiana Republican Party is doing this election cycle to promote our candidates and to let Hoosiers know about why it's time for Democrat Joe Donnelly to go!_
---
| |
---
| | **_Governor Holcomb's Week That Was_**
---
|
---
| **_Legislative Session Enters Final Stretch_** With just a few weeks left of the 2018 legislative session, many major bills are passing through the Indiana General Assembly and are heading toward Governor Holcomb's desk for review. Some of the biggest ones include:
* **Attacking the Drug Epidemic:** This week, the legislature passed one of Governor Holcomb's top priorities -- [increasing the penalty for drug dealers](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=06ff1f57e7&e=1acd9bb3d6) whose activity leads to the death of a drug user. As Governor Holcomb said, "I commend lawmakers for advancing this bill that sends a clear message to anyone who would profit from the drug crisis: If you deal or make drugs that result in the death of a Hoosier, you will be charged with a felony and go to prison for a very long time."
* **Sunday Alcohol Sales:** This week, Governor Holcomb said, "Cheers!" to Sunday alcohol sales in Indiana, [signing the bill](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=6a0b8fbf92&e=1acd9bb3d6) and legalizing carryout alcohol sales from noon to 8 p.m. each Sunday for the first time in Indiana since Prohibition.
* **New State Insect:** With the support to students across the state, [Indiana is one step closer](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=dd7b74d159&e=1acd9bb3d6) to having the Say's Firefly serve as Indiana's new state insect. This big victory wouldn't have been possible without the hundreds of students who sent letters and took an active role in the legislative process!

name: The White House
subject: Resolute Reads
body:
1600 Daily REAL NEWS PRESIDENT TRUMP DOESN'T WANT YOU TO MISS

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=5d67c202ac06323e7bf9532ad1035a89c6a4d1912b4052c59edf1196a3abc3e6cc1f6a434f688e127fda41d806e0cae234032ec8c356b7dd
'I remember your hug': Opioid victim family members thank Trump at WH conference

- Fox News Insider

At Thursday’s White House summit on opioids, President Donald J. Trump joined First Lady Melania Trump to speak with the family members of victims of America’s opioid crisis. “The father of victim Andrew Witkoff said he still remembers the embrace then-businessman Trump gave him while Andrew's family was struggling,” Fox News Insider reports.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=5d67c202ac06323e59cf73151382da45dae0eb199095fc28906bb097a9d4d9c6bda24337e0e1efa151fa0245b2eba3971397540e5659189c

Manufacturing in U.S. expands at fastest pace since May 2004

- Bloomberg Markets

American factories expanded at the fastest rate since 2004 this February, according to figures from the Institute for Supply Management. The numbers, released Thursday, show that 15 of 18 manufacturing industries indicated growth last month.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=5d67c202ac06323e20e5e305608720fa38e38acd9b89325547180f06d743e6e3b2457355065a8fb32466d9b7af34a309857226d3740d5d3c
U.S. consumer confidence rises to highest level since November 2000

- USA Today and The Associated Press

According to nonprofit organization The Conference Board, American consumers today are the most confident they have been since 2000. The business research group’s index showed that a strong job market is boosting confidence as unemployment has stayed at a 17-year low.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=5d67c202ac06323e7818b354b30bd69e0c60c3a2e52e590da09435542ad1e4854bdcb4241a04ea6eb437b8e59be264c81061d041e09fcbdf

U.S. real disposable incomes rose in January by the most in five years due to tax cuts

- Crain’s Cleveland Business and Bloomberg

“Americans’ wallets fattened in January by the most in five years” because of recent tax cuts, suggesting that greater spending power may keep boosting the U.S. economy this quarter. “The reduction in taxes helped boost the saving rate to 3.2%, the highest since August, from 2.5% in December, which was the lowest since 2007.”

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=5d67c202ac06323e8fd8e7a75741cf49df2c4a88016546d6921bd6fd80ab21bfe0e5a47dd71fd20885fa91f5150aa643415ab692edd003cc
White House defends ICE, Border Patrol from California attacks

- The Washington Times

The Trump Administration is backing U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement officials, who the mayor of Oakland, California, challenged recently by tipping off illegal immigrants of upcoming sweeps by Federal authorities. Vice President Mike Pence called federal immigration officials “heroes” at the Department of Homeland Security’s 15th anniversary event this week.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=5d67c202ac06323e070eed1d1fa852c52d3b2f68f085dd294bfb3ac9dc621a3867f2c33297d803e1589d816e9ba8408b91325e57023f6abb
Trump calls for 'very strong' penalties for drug dealers

- The Hill

While speaking at the White House’s opioids summit this week, President Trump called for America to be “very strong on penalties” given to drug dealers and pushers. body:
Hi there!

Wanted to add a bit to the note my team sent yesterday.

We spend a lot of our time diving into the positions and background of our opponents, and every day, I become more convinced that we really need to be prepared for the general election - so that Tim can continue to fight for all Virginians. [If you have a minute, read and see for yourself how important it is that we build up funds now for the general election in November >>](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=WrsAvfZJ4UpMIIl0Ou4YJDoG2RBPfgzM)
* * *
**Here 's a quick breakdown -**

Our Republican opponents continue to race to the right, but really, there's not much daylight between them all. Each of their agendas will result in the same disasters for Virginia:
* Raise middle class taxes and explode the deficit,
* Take health care away from millions and raise premiums for thousands of Virginia families,
* And divide our communities with inflammatory comments.

Meanwhile, Tim is focused on helping Virginia families, and is willing to break through the gridlock of Washington to:
* Grow our economy so salaries can increase,
* Give Virginians more health care options for less money,
* And protect our Commonwealth and country from threats at home and abroad, while bringing people together - not driving them apart.

Every time Tim is in the office, he reminds us that we cannot take this election for granted. We need to be prepared for whoever and whatever is thrown our way after the Republican primary on [June 12th.](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=OLGS6EKB9VtkMWX8RoLKjE8IkWkWUILq) If you agree that we need to keep Tim in office so he can continue to strengthen our Commonwealth, will you consider donating to his re-election campaign? (Every bit will go into defending his seat from one of those GOP extremists - I promise!) [Chip in here >>](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=AkidGskxElkL5JRgy2QLT08IkWkWUILq)

Thanks for being on Team Kaine!

_Megan_

Megan Apper
Research Director
Kaine for Virginia

name: Sara Imam via Democrats.org
subject: I'm a Parkland student survivor
body:
On February 14th, I lost 17 members of my community in a mass shooting at my school, Marjory Stoneman Douglas. But we, the survivors, refuse to let their names be forgotten. We refuse to let their dea
---
On February 14th, I lost 17 members of my community in a mass shooting at my school, Marjory Stoneman Douglas. But we, the survivors, refuse to let their names be forgotten. We refuse to let their deaths be in vain.

We have decided to take action to ensure that an event like this will never happen again. We are calling for stricter gun laws. We are calling for change.

I turned 18 the day after the shooting, and I immediately registered to vote. But many of my fellow survivors can't vote yet, and we are counting on you to stand up for young people at the ballot box.

This week the Democratic Party launched IWillVote -- a national campaign to organize voters and get them to the polls. Voting is the first step toward ending gun violence, so I'd like to ask you to do something important today.

**Will you add your name right now to commit to vote this fall?**

**[COMMIT TO VOTE](https://my.democrats. "https://my.democrats.org/page/s/IWillVote?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+COMMIT+TO+VOTE&utm_campaign=em_20180303_si_nd&source=em_20180303_si_nd")**
Students like me are protesting, marching, and speaking out for our right to live free from the fear of gun violence. We are talking to our elected officials and debating the president himself to demand new gun laws that will keep us safe.

We've kept up the momentum of our #NeverAgain movement -- but the truth is many of us are not old enough to vote.

We need you, the people of America, to get out and vote this year -- for you, your family, your community, but also for people like me. Do it for the survivors who can't vote yet. Do it for those who will never be able to vote because they were murdered in their school, their neighborhood, or their place of worship. Do it so no parent will ever have to receive the news that their child has been shot at school.

There is too much at stake for us to retreat from this fight. Can I count on you to commit to vote this fall?

**[Add your name right now to commit to vote in the fall. It's up to every single one of us to organize our friends, family, and neighbors to go to the polls.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052275f/4067b0a/57d0af8e9/7e5616ab/4076351698/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZtYVhKemRHNWhiV1VsSlE9PSI6IkRlcmVrIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IldpbGxpcyIsIkpTVmxiV0ZwYkNVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIiwiSlNWNmFYQWxKUT09IjoiMjAxMTIifQ==/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/s/IWillVote?&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+Add+your+name+right+now+to+commit+to+vot&utm_campaign=em_20180303_si_nd&source=em_20180303_si_nd")**

I will never lose my sense of outrage over our country's gun violence problem -- and I will never stop fighting to make sure our elected officials do something about it. I hope you won't either.

In solidarity,

Sara

Sara Imam
Marjory Stoneman Douglas Student and Survivor

Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, 430 South Capitol Street SE, Washington DC 20003 and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If this isn't the best email address at which to reach you, [update your contact information](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052275f/4067b0a/57d0af8e9/7e5616a8/4076351698/VEsF/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/user/manage?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=3+-+update+your+contact+information&utm_campaign=em_20180303_si_nd&source=em_20180303_si_nd"). Our email list is the best way we have of staying in regular contact with supporters like you across the country and letting you know about the work Democrats are doing. If you like staying in touch, but want to receive only the most important messages, click [here](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052275f/4067b0a/57d0af8e9/7e5616a9/4076351698/VEsC/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/signup/less-email?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=4+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180303_si_nd&source=em_20180303_si_nd"). Click [here](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052275f/4067b0a/57d0af8e9/7e5616b6/4076351698/VEsD/ "http://www.democrats.org/unsubscribe?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=5+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180303_si_nd&source=em_20180303_si_nd") to unsubscribe from our supporter list, but if you leave, it will be harder for you to stay involved in the organization that you've been such a critical part of. This organization is powered by you, and we'd love to hear your ideas. Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback [here](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052275f/4067b0a/57d0af8e9/7e5616b7/4076351698/VEsA/ "http://my.democrats.org/page/s/contact-the-democrats?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=6+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180303_si_nd&source=em_20180303_si_nd"), or just reply to this email! Thanks for supporting our party.

name: Gina Raimondo
subject: Taking action to keep Rhode Islanders safe
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Gina Raimondo
body:
Friend,
We've had to order the flags to half-staff nine times because of mass shootings while I've been Governor. We need faith and prayers, but we also need action. Last year, I signed legislation to remove guns from convicted abusers. On Monday, I was the first Governor in the nation to sign an executive order establishing a Red Flag policy to help keep guns out of the hands of potential mass shooters. In the months ahead, we need to keep up the pressure for a statewide ban on assault weapons like the ones used to gun down innocent victims in Parkland, Las Vegas, Orlando and at Sandy Hook Elementary School.
It's time for us to take action to make Rhode Island safer for our children and our communities.
Gina

name: Bob Casey
subject: Lamb is within striking distance
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bob Casey for Senate
body:
The pressure is on, and time is running out.

**The Cook Political Report just upgraded the PA-18 Special Election on March 13th to TOSS-UP.** This is huge, but there is still work to be done.

**_[For Conor Lamb, flipping this seat from red to blue is now within striking distance. But with the election in just over a week, we need to do everything in our power to help Conor cross the finish line. Can you rush $5 or more to help Conor Lamb?](https://secure.bobcasey.com/page/m/661e6a7e/51dbcc2a/95248c9/3a36735/105236867/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoicnBjNGNsIiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJlbV8yMDE4MDMwM19mcl9uZF9zMSIsImUzc2dURkJCUVZGMVpYSjVVM1J5YVc1bklIMTkiOiI1LDI1LDUwLDEwMCwyNTAsNTAwIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/rpc4cl?refcode=em_20180303_fr_nd_s1&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")_**

Conor is a dedicated public servant. He has fought for our nation in the Marine Corps and for justice as a federal prosecutor, and now he wants to go to Washington to fight for the people of Southwestern Pennsylvania.

In Congress, Conor will fight for the Democratic ideals that we all share. He will expand access to affordable healthcare, combat the opioid crisis, and make sure all of our children have an opportunity to receive a quality education. He will protect Medicare and Social Security, and he will fight to grow wages and create good jobs for the people of Pennsylvania.

Democrats need to take back Congress in 2018, and Conor could be the first step to victory. I know we can do this if we all work together.

**_[Pitch in $5 or more today for Conor Lamb and help Democrats take back Congress in 2018.](https://secure.bobcasey.com/page/m/661e6a7e/51dbcc2a/95248c9/3a36735/105236867/VEsE/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoicnBjNGNsIiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJlbV8yMDE4MDMwM19mcl9uZF9zMSIsImUzc2dURkJCUVZGMVpYSjVVM1J5YVc1bklIMTkiOiI1LDI1LDUwLDEwMCwyNTAsNTAwIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/rpc4cl?refcode=em_20180303_fr_nd_s1&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")_**

Thanks,

Bob

name: Bernie Sanders
subject: Sign my petition if you agree: "It is long past time for Congress to exercise its constitutional authority on matters of war, and that if the United States is going to participate in the Saudi-led war in Yemen, there must be a debate and a vote. Otherwise, our involvement is unauthorized and unconstitutional, and it must end."
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Bernie Sanders
body:
Our role in actively supporting the war in Yemen is a moral stain on our nation.
#
Friends -
In the midst of Trump's tweets, the dysfunctionality of Congress and the general craziness of Washington, it's easy for the media and the public to lose sight of some enormously important issues.
And one of those issues is the growing global arms race, increased saber rattling and the large number of armed conflicts taking place around the world. Billions of people on the planet today live in abysmal poverty, while country after country spends more on weapons of destruction. The military industrial complex becomes richer, while poor children die of easily prevented diseases.
One of the reasons I voted against the recent budget agreement was that, with very little attention, it increased military spending by $165 billion over two years. Even in Washington, that's a lot of money.
**Meanwhile, with very little notice, the United States is actively participating in a morally indefensible war in Yemen that most Americans know nothing about and that Congress has never voted to authorize.**
Thanks to this Saudi-led war and blockade, the people of Yemen, one of the poorest countries in the world, are undergoing unimaginable suffering:
Already, as a result of this war, some 10,000 civilians have been killed, 40,000 wounded and 3 million have been displaced. More than 15 million people lack access to clean water and sanitation, and there are more than 1 million suspected cases of cholera reported - potentially the worst outbreak in the history of the world. More than two-thirds of the population needs some kind of humanitarian support. The World Health Organization has called the situation in Yemen the world's largest humanitarian crisis as the country faces famine.
**Our role in supporting this humanitarian catastrophe is a moral stain on our nation, only made worse by the fact that Congress has actively abdicated its constitutional duty to debate and vote on our direct involvement.**
That is why I have joined with U.S. Senator Mike Lee, a conservative Republican from Utah, and U.S. Senator Chris Murphy, a Democrat from Connecticut, to introduce a bill that would require Congress to vote on U.S. participation in the war in Yemen. We cannot continue to become involved in wars just because a president wants to. It is very clear that under Article I, Section 8, clause 11 of the United States Constitution, it is the Congress that has the sole power to declare war, not the executive branch. The time is long overdue for Congress to reassert that authority, which is what our resolution does.
If Congress does not authorize U.S. participation in this war, our bill would require our involvement to end.
If the president or members of Congress believe our participation in the Saudi-led war in Yemen is in the interest of the United States, then let them come to the floor of the Senate and the House and make their case, and then let's have a vote. Otherwise, our involvement is unauthorized and unconstitutional, and it must end.
**Add your name if you agree:**
**[Sign my petition if you agree: "It is long past time for Congress to exercise its constitutional authority on matters of war, and that if the United States is going to participate in the Saudi-led war in Yemen, there must be a debate and a vote. Otherwise, our involvement is unauthorized and unconstitutional, and it must end."](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/yemen?source=em180303-t1-full "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/yemen?source=em180303-t1-full")**
****[SIGN THE PETITION](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/yemen?source=em180303-t1-full "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/yemen?source=em180303-t1-full")****
I believe that Congress has become far too comfortable with the United States engaging in military interventions around the world. Our job is to work within the international community to forge diplomatic solutions to international conflicts, not engage in more wars.
We have now been in Afghanistan more than 17 years - the longest war in the history of the United States.
We have troops in Syria under questionable authority and this administration has indicated that they may broaden that effort even further.
There should be no issue of more importance to members of Congress than the question of when it is appropriate to send the young people of our country into harm's way, knowing that some of them may not return alive.
**And it should also go without saying that every armed conflict that the United States engages in must be lawfully approved by Congress.**
I believe, along with my colleagues who have introduced this legislation, that Congress has not declared or authorized military force in Yemen, and as such our involvement is unconstitutional and unauthorized. It is my view that, without that authorization, our engagement in Yemen should be restricted to providing desperately needed humanitarian aid and diplomatic efforts to resolve the conflict.
**Add your name if you agree:**
**[Please sign my petition if you agree: "It is long past time for Congress to exercise its constitutional authority on matters of war, and that if the United States is going to participate in the Saudi-led war in Yemen, there must be a debate and a vote. Otherwise, our involvement is unauthorized and unconstitutional, and it must end."](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/yemen?source=em180303-t1-full "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/yemen?source=em180303-t1-full")**
I believe that as we get closer to a debate and a vote on this issue it's imperative that Congress know the American people are paying attention. So your voice means a lot. Thank you for making it heard.
In solidarity,
Bernie Sanders

****[ADD YOUR NAME](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/yemen?source=em180303-t1-full "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/yemen?source=em180303-t1-full")****

name: Sherrod Brown
subject: Fix the pension crisis
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Sherrod Brown
body:
|
---

| | Ohio is facing a worsening pension crisis. Our four largest public pension plans are _largely underfunded._ **And unless we do something about it, more than 60,000 Ohioans and 1.5 million workers and retirees around the country could be affected.** I'm fighting to make sure we fulfill our obligations to retired teachers, police officers, firefighters, and public employees. [**_Add your voice to mine -- demand Congress take the pension crisis seriously._**](http://oh.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302fe4f4/4462dc26/4f203ec9/20008968/1335834726/VEsH/ "http://oh.sherrodbrown.com/e180303") We're in this mess because Wall Street's greed plunged our economy into the worst recession since the Great Depression. Washington came to Wall Street's rescue -- but then Wall Street turned around and stole the pensions hardworking Ohioans earned. Ohio retirees have worked their entire adult lives. It's fundamentally unfair for us to turn our backs on them and make massive cuts to their pensions because Wall Street took big gambles to increase their own profits. [**_If our nation truly values a hard day 's work, we need to secure retirees' pensions without denying them a single cent from the benefits they've earned. Add your name if you agree._**](http://oh.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302fe4f4/4462dc26/4f203ec9/20008968/1335834726/VEsE/ "http://oh.sherrodbrown.com/e180303") With gratitude, Sherrod
